headlineSummary: Anthropic drew the most negative discussion, with direct criticism of Claude’s output and trustworthiness outweighing a mixed note, while OpenAI stayed mixed on limited evidence and Microsoft Copilot got a small positive mention [E1][E2][E5][E3].

ranking.summary: Anthropic skewed negative on medium evidence: one comment said Claude-generated code had nothing innovative, another said it would not be trusted for a system change, and a third was mixed because it praised some code while criticizing repeated unwanted edits [E1][E2][E5].
OpenAI was mixed and neutral overall on limited evidence, with one comment saying Codex would not be trusted for a task and another saying it was loved for managing servers [E2][E4].
Microsoft Copilot had a small positive mention on limited evidence, with one commenter saying it was really good for the AI era [E3].

evidence.excerpt: compiler entirely written by Claude AI. Lattner found nothing innovative in the code generated by AI [1].

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Claude AI and says its generated code had 'nothing innovative,' which is a clear criticism of model output quality.

evidence.excerpt: Would I trust Claude to switch my audio stack from Pulseaudio to Pipewire on Ubuntu? Would I trust Codex to install Hyprland on Fedora so I can test out the session? No, in fact I would not trust any agent to do any of those things on any other operating system.

annotations.rationale: The speaker explicitly says they would not trust Claude to perform the task described, indicating negative trust.
The speaker explicitly says they would not trust Codex to perform the task described, indicating negative trust.

evidence.excerpt: It's also great for the AI era, copilot is really good with that stuff.

annotations.rationale: In an AI tooling context, 'copilot' most plausibly refers to GitHub Copilot/Microsoft Copilot, and the commenter says it is 'really good' for this use.

evidence.excerpt: Nowadays I love it, since I can let Codex manage the servers for me.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names Codex and presents using it to manage servers as a reason they 'love' the setup, which is positive sentiment toward Codex's usefulness.

evidence.excerpt: Claude Code is great at writing some of the code but it keeps adding tombstones back to my remove ops because “research requires tombstones for correctness”.

annotations.rationale: The comment gives both praise ('great at writing some of the code') and criticism (it keeps making an unwanted change), so the overall sentiment is mixed.
